---
title: "System Transparency: How SPARMVET thinks"
---

When interacting with massive biological or economical datasets, certain operations take time. Understanding *why* SPARMVET pauses or reloads will save you frustration and help you understand what is "Normal" versus "Abnormal" behavior.

### The Golden Rule: Don't Panic!
**You cannot permanently break this application.**
SPARMVET operates entirely on "copies" of your recipes and data during your session. The worst-case scenario if you make a massive mistake in the Wrangle Studio is that the page freezes. If that happens:
1. Reload your browser page.
2. Select your project again from the sidebar.
You will never delete or corrupt the system's baseline data.

---

## 1. The Caching System (Tier 1 & Tier 2)

### What it is
Rather than reloading 50,000 rows of an Excel file every time you click a button, SPARMVET implements a **Cache** (specifically called Parquet Caches for Tiers 1 and 2). 

Think of caching like a restaurant prep-kitchen. Instead of chopping an onion specifically every time a customer orders a burger, the onion is chopped once in the morning (Tier 1 Ingestion) and saved in a container. Every burger made later that day (Tier 3 visualizations) just grabs the pre-chopped onion instantly.

### Why you care
The very first time you upload a dataset, SPARMVET must "chop the onion." It converts your raw, slow data into a highly optimized format.
- **Normal Behavior:** The first upload takes up to 30-40 seconds.
- **Normal Behavior:** Any subsequent visualization using that data will appear almost instantly.

## 2. Manifest Triggers: Why things recalculate

A **Manifest** dictates the baseline state of your data (Tier 2). It tells the system *how* the data should initially look before you start playing with it in the dashboard.

If you edit your `.yaml` manifest file and upload it...
- **Normal Behavior**: The screen dims with an "In Calculation" overlay.
- **Why?**: The system has to throw out the old "pre-chopped onions" and prepare new ones based on your new recipe. This wipes the Tier 2 cache and rebuilds it. It takes time, but ensures your visuals perfectly match your new rules.

### Fast vs Slow Interactions
- **Fast:** Changing a dropdown to filter a plot, or tweaking a plot color in the Tier 3 Wrangle Studio. This relies on the ultra-fast cache.
- **Slow:** Executing a `Join` command, or uploading a completely new dataset. This requires rebuilding the foundation.

## 3. Detecting Abnormal Behavior (When to ask for help)

While loading screens and dimming are normal, the following are *abnormal* and might require assistance:

- **The "Red Error Box" loops indefinitely:** If the system catches a Malformed Manifest (e.g. you used a tab instead of a space in YAML), it will give you a tip (e.g. `SPARMVET_Error: Invalid whitespace`). If fixing the file and re-uploading still causes endless loops, contact support.
- **Visuals completely vanish:** If you apply a filter in the Wrangle Studio (e.g. filtering for `Meropenem`) and the plot completely vanishes without an error message, it is highly likely you made a typo (e.g. your data actually says `meropenem`). Check your spelling! (See Tidy Data guide).

Understanding these mechanics ensures you spend less time panicking, and more time uncovering insights in your data.

---

## 4. The Unified Home Theater (v2.0 — Phase 21)

SPARMVET's main results area — the **Home** view — is organized entirely by your project's manifest. There is no separate "Analysis Theater" mode; all analytical navigation happens in one place.

### How tabs are built

Your manifest defines **Analysis Groups** (e.g., "Quality Control", "AMR Analysis"). Each group becomes a top-level tab. Within each tab, individual plots become **sub-tabs**. Everything is auto-generated from your YAML — if you add a new plot to your manifest, it appears immediately without changing any application code.

### The Tier Toggle

A small strip of buttons above the plot area controls which "layer" of the data you are looking at:

| Button | What you see |
|---|---|
| **T1 Raw** | The pre-summarised anchor data (read-only reference). |
| **T2 Reference** | The blueprint view exactly as the manifest specifies (read-only reference). |
| **T3 Wrangling** *(advanced)* | Your personal sandbox, after your wrangling steps have been applied. |
| **T3 Plot** *(advanced)* | Your sandbox data, sliced exactly to what the active plot uses. |

T3 options are only visible if your user profile allows sandbox access.

### Filters adapt to what you are looking at

When you switch between sub-tabs (e.g., from "QC Reads Barplot" to "Assembly Quality Dotplot"), the filter controls on the left automatically update to show **only the columns relevant to that plot**. You will never be presented with a filter for a column that does not appear in the current visualization.

### Comparison Mode *(advanced personas)*

If your user profile allows it, a **Comparison Mode** toggle splits the theater into two columns: the T2 blueprint reference on the left and your T3 sandbox on the right — both plots and data tables side by side — so you can see exactly what effect your wrangling steps have had.

### Collapsing sections

Both the **plot area** and the **data preview table** are independently collapsible. Click the accordion header to hide one and focus on the other. Your collapse preferences are remembered as you navigate between sub-tabs.

### Row Filters (left sidebar — Filters panel)

The **Filters** accordion in the left sidebar lets you narrow down what is plotted and previewed, without touching the underlying data.

**How it works:**

1. Select a column from the dropdown (scoped to the active plot's dataset).
2. Choose an operator: `∈ any of` / `∉ none of` for categories; `=`, `≠`, `>`, `≥`, `<`, `≤` for numbers.
3. Select or type a value, then click **+ Add** to stage the row.
4. Repeat for as many conditions as you need — rows on the same column use AND logic.
5. Click **Apply** to push the filter to the plots and data preview.
6. Click **Reset** to clear all filters.

The column selector for the data preview (above the table) lets you show or hide columns in the preview only — this does not affect the plot.

> **Note:** If you are an advanced user with T3 active, an "Apply to recipe" button will appear — this saves the filters as a step in your wrangling recipe (feature in progress).

### Exporting your results (System Tools panel)

The **System Tools** accordion in the left sidebar contains an **Export Bundle** button. This produces a self-contained `.zip` file with everything you need to reproduce and share your results:

| Contents | What it contains |
|----------|-----------------|
| `plots/` | All plots as SVG (web) or PNG ≥600 DPI (publication) |
| `data/` | T1 and T2 datasets as TSV; T3 if your profile allows it and T3 is active |
| `recipes/` | The YAML wrangling/assembly files for your project |
| `report.qmd` | A Quarto source file — run `quarto render report.qmd` to produce an HTML or PDF report |
| `FILTERS.txt` | A trace of any active filters at the time of export |
| `README.txt` | Summary of what is in the bundle |

**To export:**

1. Type your name in the "Your name" field (no spaces — used in the filename).
2. Choose a preset: **Web / Presentation** (SVG, smaller file) or **Publication** (PNG, ≥600 DPI).
3. Click **📦 Export Bundle** and save the file.

> **No Trace No Export:** If you have active row filters when you export, a `FILTERS.txt` file is automatically included in the bundle documenting exactly what was filtered. This is by design — your results must always be traceable.

### The Audit Stack (advanced personas only)

If your user profile grants sandbox access, a **Pipeline Audit** panel appears on the right side of the screen. It shows:

- **Violet nodes** — the T2 blueprint steps (inherited from the manifest, read-only reference).
- **Yellow nodes** — your personal T3 additions (filters, column selections, etc.).

For simpler user profiles, this panel is hidden entirely and the plot area expands to fill the full screen width.
